sections.title: When talked to again before going to investigate

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: How goes the investigation, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it at the moment.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: No rush, [Fremennik name]. I'm sure it's nothing to worry about. Just talk to Freygerd and Reeso at the sandpit when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: As a matter of fact [Fremennik name], I do have a quest for you. Are you interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready for another adventure!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Excellent, now-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what will it be this time? Joining warring islands?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I'm a fan of your enthusiasm, [Fremennik name], but-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you have me slay a mighty daggermouth? Or do you need my expertise in conquering untamed lands?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Nothing quite that fancy I'm afraid, [Fremennik name]. I just need you to investigate a minor disturbance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Freygerd and Reeso run the sandpit a little north of here. They mentioned they heard some rustling by their hut last night and wanted an official to investigate.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You've proven yourself more than worthy of our people's trust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that doesn't sound so bad. I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Thank you, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Very well. Return when you wish to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Reeso, I'm [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Greetings, [Fremennik name]. I imagine the Chieftain sent you over. My wife, Freygerd, is probably the best person to speak to. She was the one who saw the... well whatever it was.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: I looked about outside but couldn't see anyone out there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to the bottom of it. Thank you Reeso.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Freygerd, I'm [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Thank goodness you've arrived!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Chieftain told me that you saw some sort of disturbance outside your hut?

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Yes, my husband thinks I'm going a bit mad but I know what I saw! Red eyes, piercing with a terrifying stare. I was so frightened, I fell to the floor!

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: It was the middle of the night, so I couldn't make out what it was. But when I got back on my feet, it wasn't there anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I'll have a look around and see what I can find.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Thank you for your help.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: How's it going, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Try not to worry too much, I'm sure it's nothing.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Have you looked around the hut yet, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still think I'm missing something. I'll let you know when I'm done.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Thanks again for your help. Make sure you bring anything you find back. I'll make sure my husband delivers it to the Chieftain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This seems to be a small sack filled with venom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This doesn't look safe... Good job my backpack magically stops things from breaking!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This seems to be the fang of a relatively small creature. It's absolutely soaked in venom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's dripping with venom. I'd better make sure I don't prick my finger!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I can honestly say I wasn't expecting that. Looks like it dropped a letter of some sorts. I'd better grab it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've found everything I need for this investigation. I should let Freygerd know.

sections.title: Search the sandpit again after killing the Basilisk Youngling

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have the letter from this sand pit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that's where that letter went! How convenient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: How's it going, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've found everything I need.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Ah, you'd better talk to the wife then. She'll want to know what you've discovered.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Have you looked around the hut yet, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had a look around and found a few things I'd like to show the Chieftain. The thing that made the noise outside your home is gone now, so there's nothing to worry about.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Thank goodness! I'll have my husband take those items to the Chieftain for you.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Yeah, yeah... alright!

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Thank you so much for your help, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. I best be getting back to the Chieftain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Reeso.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Greetings, [Fremennik name]. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't by any chance read that letter, did you?

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: The sand business is booming over here in Rellekka! I don't have time to worry about some letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for letting me know.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: No problem, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, do tell me more about the sand business in Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: An adventurer like you? Don't you have anything better to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not at all. I seem to have all day to talk to people. Why else would I start a conversation with you?

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Well, being so close to the shore, Rellekka provides a lot of seaweed alongside my sand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more!

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Well I work for a business known as Sandy Sand Co. A brilliant bunch of people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: The seaweed from Rellekka, in combination with my sand business, provides a huge amount of glass for the surrounding areas!

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: Fellow Fremenniks aren't too pleased with my outside dealings, though know how important my job is. I think they let it slide for that reason.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thank you so much for all of that information Reeso. See you later.

sections.title: Freygerd after telling her about the basilisk

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: A basilisk! In our town! This is how it starts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How what starts?

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: The end!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that sounds cheery. You have nothing to fear though, not with me around. Speaking of which, I'd better get back to it.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: You didn't say, [Fremennik name]. What exactly was it that was outside? My husband didn't tell me what he took to the Chieftain for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a basilisk.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: What did you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I said it was a basilisk.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: A basilisk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I really must get back to what I was doing. Lovely to catch up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was nothing to worry about! Just a couple of knick knacks probably dropped by some kids!

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: What about the red eyes I saw though?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were probably just firestarters. That's what I found outside.

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: Well that's certainly a relief. Thank you for letting me know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a problem!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name], I'm glad you've returned! This letter you have found is very worrying and requires our utmost attention.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Never did we think this day would come.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So many lives lost. So many sacrifices.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: But the Fremennik people shall not bow to the Jormungand again!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name], please read the letter and talk to me afterwards with any questions you may have.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name], I'm glad you've returned! This letter you have found is very worrying and requires our utmost attention.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand. What does the letter mean? Who is the Jormungand? And since when could basilisks read and write?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: While they may not show it, basilisks are much smarter than they seem. Many years ago, a great basilisk army enslaved our people and took our home.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The Jormungand, sometimes known as The Serpent King, was the leader of this army. Along with his three generals, Typhor, Vritra and Bakuna, he brought a terror to these lands unlike any other.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If it hadn't been for the bravery of a great hero, we might never have been freed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A hero?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes. He was known as V. He stood up to the Serpent King and confronted him on the Island of Stone. It is said that the two of them fought for days, but in the end, V stood victorious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened next?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well that depends who you ask. The bards wrote a great song about V's victory. They called it the Ballad of the Basilisk, and it claims V killed the Jormungand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well... bards are known to bend the truth a bit, especially if it makes a song sound better. In reality, V sealed the Serpent King in a magical prison on the island, where he remains to this day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A magical prison? I thought the Fremennik people didn't approve of magic?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: V was different. Stories tell of how he unlocked the potential of magic with the single touch of a stone. In that moment, he became more than just a man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A god?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It's not a distinction the Fremennik particularly care for. To us, he has always been a hero to be respected, not a god to be worshipped. They say he felt the same way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he earned the right to use magic?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Exactly. Now the Moon Clan on the other hand, did not earn that right. Their use of magic is an insult to our people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what can we do about the Jormungand?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We will stay strong, [Fremennik name]. We'll...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We'll be doomed!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Our army isn't large enough to hold back the forces of the Serpent King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can help!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name], you're strong. But you're no army.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're forgetting Brundt. We have the element of surprise! They don't know we're aware of their attack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We could have days to prepare before they make their move. We could...

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: Chieftain Brundt!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Agnar? What is it?

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: Chieftain Brundt! I'm here on behalf of the Fremennik Council! We've heard some disturbing news about a basilisk in town.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well I won't deny there's been a little issue but [Fremennik name] and I will soon have things back under control.

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: [Fremennik name]? When will you see that this outerlander is the cause of all of our problems!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name] is no outerlander! He passed our trials and is a Fremennik, just like you and I.

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: You can disguise it all you want, but he is an outerlander and always will be! Ever since we let him in, we've had nothing but trouble!

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: And to top it all off, now we have basilisks wandering our town. The council have had enough!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: What are you saying Agnar?

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: I have a declaration here signed by seven of our twelve council members. We declare this outerlander an exile!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This is outrageous! [Fremennik name] is one of us!

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: Do not use that name! The decision of the council is final. As Chieftain, you are honour bound to enact it.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Exile, you must leave our province. Our people will no longer see you as their brother.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Leave at once. Do not attempt to interact with myself or my people.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Begone exile, you are a Fremennik no more.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Leave at once. Do not attempt to interact with myself or my people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Askeladden.

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Hey pal... listen. I shouldn't really be talking to you. Everyone is saying you're a bad person. They say you were exiled!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's true but I did nothing wrong; don't believe them Askeladden!

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: I believe you buddy! I just can't really be seen talking to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fine, I don't want to get you in trouble. Can you do me a favour though?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind.

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Okay pal. If you need anything, come back to me but keep it on the down low.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Ah! Exile! Begone with you, before you suffer the same fate as these fledglings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I was going anyway.

lines.speaker: Bjorn/Eldgrim
lines.text: Gerr owtta 'ere befur ay mayke yew!

lines.speaker: Bjorn/Eldgrim
lines.text: Behgawn exshiled. Elsh'll cahll thur gards!

lines.speaker: Bjorn/Eldgrim
lines.text: Yoo shuwdn't be warking 'round alown whyle exshiled!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there! Who are you?

lines.speaker: Falo the Bard
lines.text: Hi there exile. I am Falo the bard! Like my brother Olaf, who lives in Rellekka, I also write music.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't seem to care too much about me getting exiled.

lines.speaker: Falo the Bard
lines.text: Well we're not in Rellekka down here so I don't think it matters too much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. So, do you write about anything in particular?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Forgive me exile, though I can't allow you to board my ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please Jarvald, it's really important!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Very well... for old times sake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Do you wish to return to Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Very well, but do make sure you're not spotted leaving my ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Welcome back, [Fremennik name]. Do you have any questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't seem to be bothered about my exile.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: I'm not. Neitiznot does not consider you an exile, that's just the mainland.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hail the conquering hero!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heh. Thanks - I was pretty heroic even if I say so myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do know I've been exiled right? You don't seem to care.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Don't worry [Fremennik name], your exile does not cover the island of Jatizso. We still consider you a Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not you too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aw...

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: You're lucky you're needed on Miscellania exile. Just don't speak to me on the journey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Don't talk to me exile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, I must get to Miscellania.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: You're lucky you're needed there. Just don't speak to me on the journey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Let's go exile. Sooner you're off my ship the better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Can I get a ride on your ship?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Fine. I won't pretend to like it though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go!

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Okay. Suit yourself.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: Exile. You don't have many friends around here at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you feel about it?

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: Doesn't bother me. Some people take these things more seriously than others. On that note, do you need something?

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: Sorry [Fremennik name], I can't be seen talking to you.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: I'm not meant to be talking to you [Fremennik name]. I guess I can still take you to Ungael though if you want.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: Do you want to return to Rellekka?

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Leave exile, before I alert the guards.

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Leave now, before I get rid of you myself.

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Begone exile, you're no [brother/sister/Fremennik] to me.

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Begone exile, before I call the Fremennik guards!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: I'm sorry, you are Fremennik no more. I cannot help you. Leave, before I must call the guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that was awkward...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Indeed. I'm sorry [Fremennik name]. The reality is that many on the council did not take kindly to you becoming one of us. They've been looking for a good excuse to exile you ever since you arrived.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But they're the ones that voted for me in the first place!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well... not quite. Seven of the twelve council members gave you their vote, but you never had the support of the other five. They're the ones who've been pushing for your exile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And they've used this basilisk problem to convince some of the others.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It would seem so. Try not to judge them too harshly though, they only had what was best for the province in mind. This basilisk situation has a lot of people scared.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand though, they're only basilisks. Surely we have the strength to defeat them.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah, I see you are not familiar with the End of Things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The End of Things?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Many years ago, before the time of V, it was foretold that this world will end in a great battle. They called this battle Ragnarok, or more commonly, the End of Things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that doesn't sound good. Where do the basilisks fit in?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The prophecies say that the battle will begin after the Serpent King is freed. So you see, it is not the basilisks people fear, it is what comes after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then we'd better make sure the Jormungand stays in that prison!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, [Fremennik name], that's why I asked you to meet me out here. If you are to help us put a stop to this threat, you'll need to regain the trust of the council. And I think I know just how you can do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I think the best possible way to show your worth to them is by being the person that restores V's Shield.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: V's Shield?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The shield used by V when he defeated the Serpent King. It is legendary amongst our people. If we are to have any chance of stopping this threat, we will need to remake it.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There's a few things I know about the shield, though other things are lost from stories past.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I believe some members of the Moon Clan might know the details I don't, so your diplomatic relations should come in handy.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Keep in mind, now that you've been exiled, most people in the province will be unlikely to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. So where should I start?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Stories tell of how V's Shield was made using four components.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: First, the base of the shield. This was a traditional Fremennik Shield, like the ones our warriors use.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Next, the Lunar Glass. This was apparently created by a great Moon Clan mystic and was used to reflect the Serpent King's gaze.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Then you have the Polishing Rock. This was needed to shine the Lunar Glass. This rock was heated softly in a boiling spring.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Finally, V's Sigil. This sigil was crafted by the Moon Clan, using a unique metal known only to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find a Fremennik Shield?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Fremennik Shields are only given to warriors of the province.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made a round shield with arctic pine in my previous adventure to Neitiznot?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: No, this shield is different. I'd recommend heading to Waterbirth Island. The daggermouths within the dungeon will likely have some from warriors they've sent to Valhalla.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You could slay them and reclaim a shield from their corpses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if that doesn't work?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There's a warrior down there known as Bardur, gathering many a daggermouth hide and saving Fremennik Shields.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you have no luck finding a shield, perhaps you could charm him to give one to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Brundt, I'll keep that in mind.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do you have any more questions about V's Shield, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get Lunar Glass?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I'm not sure I'm afraid. I imagine someone from the Moon Clan would likely have more knowledge on the matter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I know just the chicken-legged-house-bound lady to ask!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry about it...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do you have any more questions about V's Shield, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make the Polishing Rock?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well firstly, a rock would probably help...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know a guy! Well, a boy. Hopefully he'll still be willing to speak to me despite the exile.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Convenient. As for the matter of heating the rock, there's some hot springs on the mountain to the east that should be warm enough.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Use something to lower the rock into one of the geysers there, and you should be good to go.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do you have any more questions about V's Shield, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make V's Sigil?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: V's Sigil is, well... in the shape of a V.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... is that it?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, one side of the sigil is shaped like a basilisk. Spines down its back, and maw wide like a gate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the other side?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Nothing fancy. Just the other side of the letter V.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly is it made of?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The stories tell of a cave on Lunar Isle where V first gained his power. Apparently it contains a unique metal used only by the Moon Clan.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I'd say three bars of this metal should be enough. Take them to an anvil and you should be able to make the sigil.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Once you have made the sigil, you'll need to have it enchanted. This was apparently done by a powerful spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know of such a spirit?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The stories didn't specify, though I would wager the Fossegrimen might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do you have any more questions about V's Shield, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Ah! Exile! Begone with you, before you suffer the same fate as these fledglings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bardur, wait! I'm here with a task from the Chieftain.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: I'm not too certain I should trust you! What is it you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking to remake V's Shield, but I require a Fremennik Shield from a warrior. Where could I find one?

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: You already have one! Begone, exile!

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: The daggermouths found in these caves should drop a few when slain. They take them from fallen warriors. I have a few myself, in fact. I wish to give them back to the families of the fallen.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: These fledgelings won't drop anything you're looking for, though. Search the caves for some matured daggermouths.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your time, Bardur.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: You're lucky I'm busy exile! Things might have been different if I wasn't...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could buy one off you?

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Hmm... Well I suppose the money would be of great help to the families of the fallen. Very well, I will sell you a shield for 150,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Perhaps I misjudged you, exile. Best of luck on your adventure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's far too much, I won't pay that!

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Well I wouldn't expect an exile like you to care about the fallen. Be gone with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could honour the fallen by remaking V's Shield.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Hmm... They would indeed be pleased. Having your shield used to make the shield of a hero would be an honour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you say?

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Very well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Bardur.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Perhaps I misjudged you, exile. Best of luck on your adventure.

sections.title: Bardur after receiving a Fremennik shield from him

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Exile, has that shield served you well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, thank you.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: All the best, exile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost it.

lines.speaker: Bardur
lines.text: Well you're not having another one if that's how you take care of things! If you want another, you'll need to slay some daggermouths!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah! [player name], I'm glad to see you've returned. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you could help me make...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Lunar Glass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really need to stop doing that.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Stop doing what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop finishing my...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Sentences? Sorry it's a force of habit! Mind reading and watnot.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Oh don't look at me like that! I'm not the one who exiled you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, those are my private thoughts! You can't just go reading my mind without permission.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Those aren't private thoughts! They're nothing compared to the other things I can see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please... can we just focus on the matter at hand?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Sure thing! Lunar Glass isn't so hard to make, but I will require a piece of Molten Glass. Return to me when you have some!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank y...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Sure thing! Lunar Glass isn't so hard to make, but I will require...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some Molten Glass? I have some right here!

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Hey! That's... really annoying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, how the tables have turned!

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: How did you even know...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Anyway, let me imbue this piece of glass for you.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You will need to enchant it by using it on the Astral Altar. The process will also require 100 Astral Runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Can't you just do it for me?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Afraid not, I have lots of tidying to do around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tidying?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Have you ever tried living in a house that's constantly shaking? It's a total nightmare, believe me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I never thought about that before, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Best of luck, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah! [player name], I'm glad to see you've returned. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you could help me make...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Lunar Glass? I can do that. I will require a piece of Molten Glass. Return to me when you have some!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank y...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Lunar Glass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! I have th...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: The Molten Glass? Excellent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That one wasn't even mind reading! I could have guessed what I was going to say there.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Anyway, let me imbue this piece of glass for you.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You will need to enchant it by using it on the Astral Altar. The process will also require 100 Astral Runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Can't you just do it for me?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Afraid not, I have lots of tidying to do around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tidying?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Have you ever tried living in a house that's constantly shaking? It's a total nightmare, believe me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I never thought about that before, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Best of luck, [player name].

sections.title: Baba Yaga again after she imbues the molten glass

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah! [player name], I'm glad to see you've returned. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you could help me make...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Lunar Glass? But I can see you already have the Imbued Glass you need! You don't have to be a mind reader to see that...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Remember, you'll need to enchant it by using it on the Astral Altar. This process will also require 100 Astral Runes.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Lunar Glass? You'll need some Imbued Glass first. Don't worry, I can make some more for you.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You don't seem to have any Molten Glass. Come back when you do!

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: There you go. Remember, you'll need to enchant it by using it on the Astral Altar. This process will also require 100 Astral Runes.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Lunar Glass? But I can see you already have some! You don't have to be a mind reader to see that...

sections.title: Enchanting the molten glass (i) with Lunar glass in the player's inventory or bank

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have some Lunar Glass. I should probably gather the rest of the items for V's Shield!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It might have helped if I'd actually remembered to bring my pet rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This geyser looks a little small. I'd probably do a better job heating this rock in a larger geyser!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't do this to you with all of these happy memories!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right... I'll talk to Peer the Seer. He'll help me forget all of our times together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It'll make this easier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It might have helped if I'd actually remembered to bring my pet rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here we go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any second now, I'm going to lower this rock into the geyser and boil it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just a rock, how is it looking so sad? Come on, I can do this! For the Province!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's for the good of the Province... I must do it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't do it! I love you pet rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right, pet rock... Why does it have to be this way?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't do this to you with all of these happy memories!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right... I'll talk to Peer the Seer. He'll help me forget all of our times together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It'll make this easier.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Though I appreciate your efforts in our trials, I'm afraid I can't speak to you [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, Seer! I'm on a mission for Brundt.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Hmm... If the Chieftain can still trust you, so can I. How may I be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a bit of a sensitive subject...

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: You can tell me anything, [Fremennik name]. What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't bring myself to boil my pet rock!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, I'm a horrible person! Please help me, I can't bring myself to do it.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Are you serious? I'm a busy man...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please Seer, you're my only hope!

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Very well. I'll clear your mind of any memories you have of your...

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: *cough*

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: pet rock...

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I won't be able to speak with you after this. The others might start to get suspicious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Concussio! Forgessan! Geodeus!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Seer! I'll head back to the mountains now.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Now be gone with you, exile!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know it's just an act... but that hurt.

sections.title: Large geyser after the player has forgotten their memories with their pet rock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not going to be able to get my rock back with just my bare hands! I'll need to get something to fish it out.

sections.title: Inspecting again with another pet rock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already dropped one rock in there. I should try fishing it out rather than throwing more away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I don't think that will be bobbing up to the top anytime soon! I'll need to fish it out with something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Darn it! That rock was hot...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If only I had something that could keep my hands cold!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Darn it! It would probably help if I actually wore my ice gloves.

sections.title: Inspecting either geyser with a polishing rock in the player's inventory or bank

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have a Polishing Rock. I think I'm done here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have a Polishing Rock in my bank. I think I'm done here.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear that I can present you with an offering and you will enchant my lyre for me.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: This is true. For a simple enchantment, you may place an offering on my Altar. Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm currently trying to remake V's Shield. I was told I need his sigil enchanted by a powerful spirit.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: V? Now that's a name I haven't heard in a long time...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know of V?

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: Oh yes. Were it not for V, this conversation might never had happened.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: I owe V a debt that can never be repaid. It would be an honour to enchant his sigil.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: Bring the unenchanted sigil to me and I'll provide you with what you need.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: *The Fossegrimen whispers an enchantment*

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: There you go! Go forth mighty warrior.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I have everything needed to make V's Shield.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well what are you waiting for? Get it made and we'll have everything we need to put an end to your exile.

sections.title: Using the items on each other

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This looks complete! I should head back to the Chieftain and see what he thinks.

sections.title: Brundt, with V's shield in the bank

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've completed V's Shield!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hand it over then, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry. I simply left it in my bank. I'm such a ditzy person sometimes!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: That's certainly one way to put it... Perhaps you should go and get it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do, see you shortly!

sections.title: Brundt, with V's shield, without two kegs of beer

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Have you got the two kegs of beer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have them right here!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I'm litchrally abousht to go shtand uhp to the enshtire *hiccup* counshil for yooo...! I need thish!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a very good point. Carry on then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably head back to the longhall...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've completed V's Shield!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name]... This is incredible! It's as if you forged the shield from a picture. It's exactly how I imagined it from the stories!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think this will be enough to convince the council?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I hope so [Fremennik name]. I do suspect some of them may never respect you though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Beggars can't be choosers, I suppose. Let's get going then...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Wait!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The shield. It requires one last thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's missing?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Return to me with two beers. No... Two kegs of beer! Quickly! There's not much time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay!

sections.title: Brundt, with V's shield and two kegs of beer

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Return to me with two beers. No... Two kegs of beer! Quickly! There's not much time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I actually have some here.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You're a bigger alcoholic than I am! Hand them over.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Good to see you [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you Brundt. You sobered up quickly.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We Fremennik know how to handle a drink, [Fremennik name]. Anyway, I have some good news for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Following your successful recreation of V's Shield, the council have agreed to end your exile. I hearby declare you a Fremennik again!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Not only that, but we would like you to be the one to carry V's legendary shield into battle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm very honoured.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So you should be [Fremennik name]. V was our greatest hero. There is no greater honour than to bear his shield in battle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's our plan?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The basilisks intend to attack the town so they can keep us distracted while they free the Jormungand. Luckily for us, we know about this.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Once the attack begins, the majority of our forces will stay here to hold the town. While they do, you and I will journey to the Island of Stone.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There, we will ensure that the prison of the Jormungand remains sealed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good, when do...

lines.speaker: Fremennik Warrior
lines.text: Basilisks in the market! Basilisks in the market!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: They've walked right through our defences!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: They've walked right through our defences!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Kill them all!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Kill them all!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: This is Rellekka!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Kill the invaders!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: You die here beast!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Die you foul beasts!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Death to the basilisks!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: Kill them! Kill them all!

lines.speaker: NPC
lines.text: You will not take our home!

sections.title: If the player leaves the market during the Market fight

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name]! Get to the boat. It's time for us to go!

lines.speaker: Fremennik Warrior
lines.text: Basilisks in the market! Basilisks in the market!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What now?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Have you opened the door yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hurry [Fremennik name]. We need to get inside before it's too late.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the door unlocked.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Good work [Fremennik name]. Let's get inside and see what's going on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What now?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This isn't good [Fremennik name]. The prison has already been breached.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we're too late?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Not quite, but we'll have to act fast. If the Jormungand manages to leave the prison, the End of Things will be upon us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well we'd better get in there and make sure that doesn't happen.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It looks like the basilisks resealed the door behind them. We'll have to get through it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: From what I can tell, the door uses a combination lock. You'll need to get the right combination to unlock it. Too many wrong attempts will reset the lock though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better be careful then. I'll get to work.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hurry [Fremennik name]. We can't afford to waste any time.

sections.title: Brundt again after unlocking the door

lines.speaker: Typhor
lines.text: Humans!

lines.speaker: Bakuna
lines.text: Destroy them! Our king must be freed!

lines.speaker: Vritra
lines.text: It will be done.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: With me [Fremennik name]! Let's kill these foul beasts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What now?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Let's get inside and see what's going on.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Turn away [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Don't look into his eyes!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Don't look at him [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name]! Do not face him!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The stone is weak! You can break it!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Try to break free [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: [Fremennik name]! You need to break loose!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You can free yourself [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: The Jormungand
lines.text: At long last, I have been granted freedom. You have done well Bakuna.

lines.speaker: Bakuna
lines.text: I exist to serve.

lines.speaker: The Jormungand
lines.text: Good, for I have one final use of you. My long rest has weakened me, you will grant me strength.

lines.speaker: The Jormungand
lines.text: Power!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This isn't over yet [Fremennik name], the Jormungand is weak from his imprisonment! We can still stop him!

lines.speaker: The Jormungand
lines.text: Humans, you have been bestowed with a great honour, for you are about to become the first of your kind in millennia to feed my power!

lines.speaker: The Jormungand
lines.text: Rejoice with me, for your miserable lives are finally at an end!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You will not defeat us this day! Come [Fremennik name]!

sections.title: Brundt again after leaving the prison and coming back to the Island of Stone

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What now?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hurry [Fremennik name]! We need to get inside and stop those basilisks!

sections.title: Attempting to enter the prison before claiming items from Brundt the Chieftain

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Wait [Fremennik name]! I have some things for you!

sections.title: Talking to Brundt while he has items in his Item Retrieval Service

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any of my items?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I do have some of your items here [Fremennik name]. Do try to be careful with them.

sections.title: Brundt's right-click Collect option while he does not have items in his Item Retrieval Service

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have anything for you [Fremennik name]. If I did have any of your items, but you died before collecting them, they'll now be lost.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah, nothing like a glorious fight to make you feel alive!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Now? We drink of course! The Jormungand has been defeated and the End of Things prevented!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? They did manage to free him. How do you know we weren't too late?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You worry too much [Fremennik name]. If the end had begun, we'd have noticed by now. Come! Let us go and celebrate!

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: T'was on a haughty winter,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: We're bitten with frost,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: And none would dare venture,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Where many were lost.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: V sailed his mighty longship,

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Another great victory for our people!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: But it wouldn't have been possible without [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Agnar
lines.text: You have truly proven yourself to be one of us, [Fremennik name]! I'm sorry we ever doubted you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just glad it all worked out in the end.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: And on that note, time for a drink! I want to see if [Fremennik name] can still drink me under the table!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh...

sections.title: The off-screen segment of the bard's ballad

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: To the Island of Stone,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Where the king of the serpents,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Had made it his home.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: The storm it was a ragin',

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Waves broke on the deck,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: The bell rang with a tolling,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: To warn of the wreck.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: But naught could deter him,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: From the fate of his quest,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Winds tore through the main sail,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Yet onward he pressed.
